reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this demand trickles down to value-fashion price points

chanderi-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: External demand signals strong but internal POS shows underperformance

conflicts.title: External demand vs Internal POS underperformance
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong organic demand at moderate discount
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak emerging volume at low price
conflicts.internal_says: Similar SKU underperformed: 38% sell-through, high returns, metro-only
conflicts.buyer_question: Will a straight kurti with zari border overcome the durability and maintenance issues that hurt the A-line version?
conflicts.proves: Chanderi silk has a narrow addressable market within our store base
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a different silhouette or price point would change the outcome

conflicts.title: Meesho weak signal vs Nykaa/Myntra strong signals
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak volume, imitation product, low rating, not accelerating
conflicts.internal_says: Underperformance in tier-2/3 regions (Lucknow, Patna, Indore)
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the trend limited to premium/metro customers, or can it scale to value-conscious markets?
conflicts.proves: Demand is concentrated in higher-income segments
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a lower price point or blended fabric would unlock mass demand

convergences.title: Chanderi silk has genuine demand at premium and mass retail
convergences.detail: Nykaa and Myntra/Ajio both show strong signals with low discount distortion, indicating real style-driven demand.
convergences.proves: The trend is not a flash in the pan; consumers are willing to pay for Chanderi silk kurtis.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this demand translates to our specific store base and price point.

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra/Ajio show strong, organic demand for Chanderi silk straight kurtis with zari border, with near-zero discount on Nykaa and moderate discount on Myntra. This suggests a genuine style trend that could capture festive and office-wear demand. If executed well, a well-priced version could see strong sell-through in metro stores.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Internal POS data from a similar SKU (A-line) shows underperformance: 38% sell-through, 35% markdown, 18% return rate, and complaints about wash durability. The trend may be limited to premium customers and metro regions, with weak signals on Meesho indicating limited mass-market appeal. Our core value customer may find the fabric too delicate.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, near-zero discount, editorial backing, high ratings

myntra.reason: Strong presence, top ranks, high review velocity, low discount risk

meesho.reason: Weak presence, imitation product, low rating, not accelerating

internal.reason: Direct sales data from similar SKU, clear underperformance pattern

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: External demand signals are strong but internal POS data from a similar SKU shows clear underperformance. Recommend a small batch test in metro stores only, with a blended fabric option to address durability concerns.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a straight silhouette with zari border overcome the durability and maintenance issues that hurt the A-line version?

reasoning_trace.signal: Strong volume: 15,000+ units, 340 resellers, price ₹249, reseller growth +18%.

reasoning_trace.signal: Past similar SKU: 78% sell-through, 44% margin, 4% returns, stockouts in 45 stores.
reasoning_trace.proves: Proven winner for our base; strong repeat (34%) and low returns; under-ordered last time.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether current designs will match past performance (design fatigue risk).

reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources confirm block-print cotton kurti is a high-demand category; Meesho and Internal both show strong volume and repeat.

blockprint-cotton.headline: All sources converge on strong demand for block-print cotton kurti, but discount distortion on Myntra and price gap with Meesho create medium conflicts.

conflicts.title: Myntra high discount vs Internal full-price success
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong presence but discounts up to 30% suggest price-driven demand
conflicts.internal_says: Past SKU sold well at full price (44% margin, 8% markdown)
conflicts.buyer_question: Is Myntra's discount necessary to drive volume, or can we maintain full price based on internal history?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists across channels, but price sensitivity varies.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether our full-price strategy will work for this batch without discounting.

conflicts.title: Meesho low price vs trend price band
conflicts.internal_says: Past SKU sold at higher price point successfully
conflicts.buyer_question: Will the Meesho volume cannibalize our higher-priced offering, or does it represent a separate segment?
conflicts.proves: There is demand at both low and mid price points.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the same customer would pay ₹399-599 when ₹249 is available.

convergences.title: Block-print cotton kurti is a high-demand category
convergences.detail: Myntra strong presence, Meesho high volume, Internal past success all confirm demand.
convergences.proves: Category is proven across tiers and channels.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether current designs will repeat past performance.

convergences.title: Internal and Meesho both show strong repeat/volume
convergences.detail: Internal repeat buy rate 34%, Meesho reseller growth +18% and 15k units sold.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether Meesho buyers are one-time or repeat.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven category with strong demand across mass retail and our own stores. Internal data shows high sell-through (78%), low returns (4%), and strong repeat (34%). Meesho volume (15k+ units) confirms deep tier-2/3/4 demand. Opportunity to capture both value and mid-price segments.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Myntra discounts up to 30% suggest price sensitivity in organized retail, which may pressure margins. Meesho's ₹249 price point creates a gap vs our ₹399-599 band. Design fatigue is a risk given past success. Nykaa's absence indicates no premium upside.

nykaa.reason: Absent but expected; not a negative signal for value segment.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but high discount risk; rank and reviews are solid.

meesho.reason: High volume, many resellers, clear regional demand; price point is low but consistent.

internal.reason: Detailed past performance data; high sell-through and margin; directly relevant.

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Internal data and Meesho volume strongly support demand, but Myntra discount distortion and price gap with Meesho warrant caution. Recommend a small batch to test current designs, leveraging past success while mitigating design fatigue risk.
blockprint-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will current block-print designs achieve similar sell-through as the past SKU without heavy discounting?

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Strong premium demand; Nykaa presence is emerging, not established

reasoning_trace.proves: Moderate organized retail demand at mass price point; discount is moderate, not heavy
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven; rank is moderate, not top

reasoning_trace.proves: Strong emerging volume in price-sensitive tier-2/3/4 markets, especially Western India
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable or a fad; reseller growth is accelerating but early
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong volume signal contrasts with Nykaa's weak premium presence; aligns with Myntra's moderate signal

reasoning_trace.proves: Bandhani is a regional bet with proven demand in Western India; national rollout underperforms
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a different design/color could unlock South/East demand
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal data strongly contradicts any national bullishness; supports regional allocation

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa weak premium vs Meesho strong volume; Internal POS regional vs any national assumption
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources show some demand for Bandhani print; Myntra and Meesho both indicate moderate to strong mass-market interest

bandhani-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho and Myntra show mass-market demand, but Nykaa weak and Internal POS warns of regional-only success

conflicts.title: Nykaa weak premium vs Meesho strong volume
conflicts.nykaa_says: Bandhani print has limited premium D2C presence; only 1 product at ₹1,490 with discount
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence at ₹649 with low discount risk
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong emerging volume: 4,200+ units, 85 resellers, 35% MoM growth at ₹279
conflicts.internal_says: Past similar SKU had regional success only; national rollout failed
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand genuine style-driven or just price-driven at the mass level?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani print has strong price-sensitive demand in Western India but weak premium traction
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend can sustain at higher price points or expand nationally

conflicts.title: Internal POS regional data contradicts any national rollout assumption
conflicts.nykaa_says: Limited premium presence, no national signal
conflicts.internal_says: Past SKU failed in South and East India; only Western India performed well
conflicts.buyer_question: Should this be a regional buy for West India only?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani print demand is geographically concentrated in Western India
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a different design or marketing could unlock other regions

convergences.title: Mass-market demand exists for Bandhani print
convergences.detail: Myntra shows moderate demand at ₹649 with low discount risk; Meesho shows strong volume at ₹279 with accelerating reseller growth
convergences.proves: Bandhani print has proven demand in the mass market, especially in Western India
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable or a seasonal fad

bandhani-straight.upside_summary: Bandhani print shows strong emerging volume on Meesho (4,200+ units, 35% MoM growth) and moderate demand on Myntra at a healthy price point (₹649, 20% discount). The trend is accelerating in Western India, suggesting a regional opportunity. If executed well, a well-priced kurti could capture significant share in Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra.
bandhani-straight.catch_summary: Nykaa's weak premium presence and Internal POS data from a similar past SKU (52% sell-through, 22% markdown) indicate that Bandhani is a regional bet, not a national one. The trend failed in South and East India previously. National rollout risks high markdowns and poor sell-through. Additionally, the Meesho price point (₹279) is far below the target band (₹499-799), raising questions about price sensitivity.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found; emerging presence; no full-price signal

myntra.reason: Only 1 product; moderate rank; low review velocity

meesho.reason: Strong volume data; accelerating reseller growth; multiple regions

internal.reason: Direct past sales data with clear regional insights

bandhani-straight.bet_rationale: Bandhani print has strong regional demand in Western India, validated by Meesho and Myntra, but internal POS data warns against national rollout. A cautious buy limited to Western India stores with a small batch is recommended.
bandhani-straight.key_uncertainty: Can a modern colorway or design unlock demand in South and East India, or is Bandhani permanently regional?

reasoning_trace.signal: Strong volume: 28k+ units, 520 resellers, 12% MoM growth, price ₹399.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong price-sensitive demand in tier-2/3/4 markets; reseller growth indicates grassroots adoption.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand quality or repeat purchase intent; reviews are short and incentivized.

reasoning_trace.proves: Proven demand for kurti+palazzo sets in our stores at ₹599-999; strong repeat and low returns.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove next season's designs will maintain velocity or that design fatigue won't set in.
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal success contradicts external saturation narrative; our customer sees value in complete outfit, not just kurti.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong; Myntra high discount vs internal margin; Meesho low price vs internal price band.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All external sources (Myntra, Meesho) show strong demand; internal POS confirms demand for similar product.

fusion-palazzo.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, but Myntra/Meesho strong; internal POS confirms demand despite external saturation signals.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; trend absent at ₹800+.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong demand at ₹799-849 with heavy discount.
conflicts.internal_says: Strong demand at ₹599-999 with healthy margin.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this trend purely mass-market, or can it sustain a premium price point without discounting?
conflicts.proves: Trend is mass-market driven; premium D2C not picking up.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether premium positioning is impossible or just untapped.

conflicts.title: Myntra high discount vs internal margin
conflicts.myntra_says: High discount (35-40%) needed to drive rank.
conflicts.internal_says: Achieved 38% margin with 12% markdown; demand not discount-dependent.
conflicts.buyer_question: Will our customers still buy at full price if external channels are discounting heavily?
conflicts.proves: External demand is price-sensitive; internal demand is less so.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether internal demand will hold if external discounts become more visible.

conflicts.title: Meesho low price vs internal price band
conflicts.buyer_question: Will Meesho's lower price cannibalize our sales or expand the market?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists at multiple price points; Meesho captures value segment.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether Meesho buyers are the same as our target or different.

convergences.title: Strong demand across mass retail channels
convergences.detail: Myntra (rank 2,4) and Meesho (28k units) both show strong demand for kurti+palazzo sets.
convergences.proves: The trend has broad appeal across organized and value mass retail.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether demand is sustainable or seasonal.

convergences.title: Internal POS confirms demand for similar product
convergences.detail: Past similar SKU achieved 82% sell-through, 38% margin, and 41% repeat rate.
convergences.proves: Our customer base has proven appetite for this product format at our price point.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that new designs will replicate past performance.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: Strong demand signals from Myntra and Meesho, plus proven internal success, suggest the trend has legs. The complete-outfit value proposition differentiates from individual kurtis and drives repeat purchases (41% repeat rate). Stockouts in 68 stores indicate unmet demand in our own network.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence suggests no premium D2C traction, and Myntra's heavy discounting raises questions about price-driven demand. Meesho's low price point could cannibalize if not managed. Design fatigue is a risk given past success; next season's designs may not maintain velocity.

nykaa.reason: No data; absence may indicate trend not premium, but could also be a gap in listing.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but high discount risk; rankings may be inflated by price cuts.

meesho.reason: High volume but low rating (3.6) and incentivized reviews; reseller growth is positive but not accelerating.

internal.reason: Reliable ground truth from own stores; high sell-through, margin, and repeat rate.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: Internal POS and mass retail signals converge on strong demand, but Nykaa's absence and Myntra's discount dependency warrant caution. Recommend a small batch to test new designs, leveraging the complete-outfit value prop.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will our customers continue to buy at full price if external channels discount heavily, and can we avoid design fatigue?

ajrakh-cotton.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree on demand quality: Nykaa premium vs Meesho value, but all show early-stage signals

conflicts.myntra_says: Demand at ₹699 with zero discount suggests moderate price acceptance
conflicts.meesho_says: Demand at ₹299 with accelerating resellers suggests price-sensitive mass adoption

conflicts.cannot_prove: Which price point reflects true style preference vs price elasticity

conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether volume will sustain or translate to organized retail

convergences.proves: Ajrakh print is gaining traction but not yet mainstream

convergences.proves: Product quality and design are well-received

ajrakh-cotton.upside_summary: Ajrakh print shows genuine early demand across premium (Nykaa) and value (Meesho) channels with low discount distortion. Meesho's accelerating reseller growth (+28% MoM) suggests potential for mass adoption. Positive ratings indicate product satisfaction. If the trend gains momentum, a well-priced kurti at ₹499-799 could capture both premium and value segments.
ajrakh-cotton.catch_summary: All sources show weak volume; trend is still nascent. Meesho's volume at ₹299 may be price-driven, not style-driven. No internal POS data to validate. Nykaa's premium signal is from a single product with limited stock. Risk of overestimating demand if trend fizzles or remains niche.

nykaa.reason: Single product, limited stock, but clean signal (low discount)

myntra.reason: Single product, low rank, but zero discount and positive rating

meesho.reason: Moderate volume, accelerating growth, but price-sensitive platform

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot confirm or deny external signals

ajrakh-cotton.bet_rationale: All sources show early-stage demand with low discount distortion, but volume is too low to confirm a strong trend. A small trial in representative stores can validate demand without overcommitting.
ajrakh-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will Meesho's price-sensitive demand sustain and translate to organized retail at higher price points?

conflicts.proves: Demand exists at higher price points; Meesho weakness suggests limited appeal in price-sensitive markets
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether Meesho will pick up later or the trend is inherently premium

conflicts.title: Internal POS narrow window vs external broad signals
conflicts.nykaa_says: Editorial placement suggests wedding season, not just Diwali
conflicts.myntra_says: Rankings indicate ongoing demand beyond a single window
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak signal, no seasonal pattern evident
conflicts.internal_says: 71% sell-through but only in 3-week Diwali window; post-festive drop 90%
conflicts.buyer_question: Can the trend sustain beyond a 3-week window this year?
conflicts.proves: Organza festive kurtis are a proven short-window product
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether external signals indicate a longer season or just early festive buying

convergences.title: Premium and mass demand aligned
convergences.detail: Nykaa and Myntra both show strong demand at ₹1,200-1,900 with low discount distortion, indicating style-driven demand.
convergences.proves: Organza embroidered kurtis have genuine demand at higher price points
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this demand is sustainable or seasonal

convergences.title: Internal POS validates concept
convergences.detail: Past similar SKU achieved 71% sell-through and 48% margin, confirming the product works in a festive window.
convergences.proves: The product can be profitable if timed correctly
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the same performance will repeat this year

organza-embroidered.upside_summary: Strong demand signals from Nykaa and Myntra at premium and mass price points with low discount distortion suggest style-driven demand. Internal POS shows proven success with 71% sell-through and 48% margin in a festive window. The trend has D2C backing and editorial placement, indicating potential for a well-executed value-fashion version.
organza-embroidered.catch_summary: Meesho weakness indicates limited appeal in price-sensitive markets, suggesting the trend may not trickle down to tier-2/3/4. Internal data warns of a narrow 3-week festive window; over-committing could lead to post-festive markdowns. Nykaa's discount (15%) and lack of full-price products raise questions about true demand strength.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found; discount distorts full-price signal

myntra.reason: 2 products with high ranks, low discount, strong ratings

meesho.reason: Weak signal with low rating; data may be noisy

internal.reason: Detailed POS data from 120 stores; proven performance

organza-embroidered.bet_rationale: Strong convergence between Nykaa and Myntra indicates genuine demand at premium/mass price points, and internal POS confirms past success. However, Meesho weakness and the narrow festive window from internal data warrant a cautious approach: buy for a tight festive window, not a full season.
organza-embroidered.key_uncertainty: Will the festive window expand this year, or will demand remain concentrated in 3 weeks?

reasoning_trace.proves: Premium D2C interest exists for linen straight kurti with mandarin collar at ₹1,795
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable at full price or will trickle down to value segment
reasoning_trace.tension: Premium signal contrasts with weak Meesho and underperforming internal POS

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven; low review velocity suggests limited traction

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹399, 220+ units, 12 resellers, 3.2 rating, slow growth
reasoning_trace.proves: Price-sensitive market has weak volume and no acceleration
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether trend will gain traction in tier-2/3/4; current signal is too weak
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak Meesho contradicts any mass-market demand thesis; aligns with internal POS failure

reasoning_trace.proves: Linen kurtis underperform for our value-fashion customer due to wrinkling and returns
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a linen-blend at lower price could succeed; no data on that variant

linen-chinese-collar.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: External signals show moderate presence, but internal POS proves linen kurtis failed for your value-fashion base

conflicts.title: Internal POS contradicts external demand signals
conflicts.nykaa_says: Premium D2C has emerging interest at ₹1,795
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence at ₹799 with low discount distortion
conflicts.internal_says: Similar SKU underperformed: 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, 24% returns
conflicts.buyer_question: Why would this trend succeed now when a similar linen kurti failed in SS23?
conflicts.proves: Linen kurtis have not worked for your value-fashion customer base at scale
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a linen-blend variant or different price point could change outcome

conflicts.title: Nykaa premium vs Meesho weak: demand quality gap
conflicts.internal_says: No data on premium vs value split
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand real or just a niche premium trend that won't scale to value?
conflicts.proves: There is some demand at higher price points but not at mass-market price
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend will trickle down to ₹699-999 or remain premium-only

convergences.title: All sources show weak or problematic demand
convergences.detail: Nykaa has only 1 product with discount; Myntra/Ajio have low review velocity; Meesho weak; internal POS failed
convergences.proves: No source shows strong, clean demand for linen straight kurti with Chinese collar
convergences.cannot_prove: That the trend is completely dead; niche premium demand exists

linen-chinese-collar.upside_summary: There is some premium D2C interest at ₹1,795 and moderate organized retail presence at ₹799 with low discount distortion. If the trend gains momentum, a well-executed linen-blend version at ₹699-999 could capture some demand, especially in metro markets like Mumbai where the internal POS saw some success.
linen-chinese-collar.catch_summary: Internal POS data is the strongest signal: a similar linen kurti failed with 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, and 24% return rate. Your customer base avoids high-maintenance fabrics. Meesho shows no mass-market traction. The trend appears niche and risky for value-fashion.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found; discount applied; moderate signal strength

myntra.reason: Moderate presence but low review velocity; discount risk medium

meesho.reason: Weak volume; low rating; no acceleration; small sample

internal.reason: Directly relevant past SKU with clear outcome; high confidence for your base

linen-chinese-collar.bet_rationale: Internal POS strongly advises against a full launch, but external signals show some premium interest. A small trial of a linen-blend variant (less wrinkling) in select metro stores could test if the fabric issue is mitigated.
linen-chinese-collar.key_uncertainty: Would a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at ₹699-999 change the return rate and sell-through compared to the pure linen SKU that failed?

ikat-anarkali.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium Nykaa signal conflicts with discount-driven Myntra and ultra-low Meesho; demand quality uncertain

conflicts.title: Demand quality: full-price premium vs discount-driven mass vs ultra-low price
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at ₹1,650 with minimal discount; genuine style-driven interest
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank at ₹899 with 30% discount; demand may be price-driven
conflicts.meesho_says: Emerging volume at ₹329; price-sensitive tier-2/3/4 demand
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the ikat anarkali trend truly style-driven or is demand inflated by discounts and low price points?
conflicts.proves: Ikat anarkali has broad appeal across price segments from premium to value
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend will sustain at mid-price (₹599-899) without heavy discounting

convergences.title: Positive demand across all external sources
convergences.detail: Nykaa strong, Myntra strong, Meesho emerging — all show some level of demand for ikat print anarkali
convergences.proves: Ikat anarkali is a live trend with multi-tier appeal
convergences.cannot_prove: Depth and sustainability of demand; whether it's a fad or enduring style

convergences.title: Regional consistency in eastern/southern India
convergences.detail: Meesho regions (Odisha, AP, Telangana, WB) align with ikat's traditional roots; Nykaa's Karagiri brand also heritage-focused
convergences.proves: Regional strength in east/south; trend may have cultural resonance

ikat-anarkali.upside_summary: Ikat print anarkali shows strong premium demand on Nykaa (full-price, editorial featured) and emerging volume on Meesho (2,100+ units, 22% reseller growth). The trend has heritage appeal and regional strength in eastern/southern India. If executed well at ₹599-899 with minimal discount, it could capture both aspirational and value segments.
ikat-anarkali.catch_summary: Myntra data reveals heavy discounting (30% off) for the ikat anarkali, suggesting demand may be price-driven rather than style-driven. The wide price gap between Nykaa (₹1,650) and Meesho (₹329) creates uncertainty about the optimal price point. No internal POS data exists to validate external signals, increasing risk.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price signal, editorial backing, high rating

myntra.reason: Strong presence but high discount risk; demand quality unclear

meesho.reason: Emerging volume but price gap large; reseller data may be noisy

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot validate external signals

ikat-anarkali.bet_rationale: All external sources show positive demand, but the conflict between premium full-price and discount-driven mass suggests demand quality is uncertain. A small trial in representative stores, especially in eastern/southern regions, can validate the trend before scaling.
ikat-anarkali.key_uncertainty: Is the ikat anarkali trend driven by genuine style preference or by deep discounts and low price points?
